title: DOM node recycling pool (~40 nodes) replaces full-render _cardMap
description: Currently _cardMap (Map<trackId, HTMLElement>) keeps every track's card in the DOM. Replace with a pool of ~40 recycled DOM nodes managed by the Virtualizer (TASK-031). Cards are reassigned to different content_ids as the user scrolls.
acceptanceCriteria: Total DOM card nodes <= ~40 at any time on a 10k library.
Scrolling reuses existing nodes (assigned new content_id + re-rendered fields).
renderItem(index, node) updates only changed fields - never replaces the whole node.
Existing FLIP reorder animations only fire for true position changes inside the visible window (not for cards that scrolled out).
tests/web/virtualization.test.js asserts pool size cap + correct content_id on recycled nodes.
Verified in browser using playwright: memory snapshot shows ~stable heap during 10k scroll.

steps.details: Module-level: _cardPool = [] (array of DOM nodes); _visibleIndex = new Map(). On first render, create N nodes upfront (clone from a template fragment).

Per-call: get track = filteredTracks()[index]; update node.querySelector('.title').textContent = track.title; same for artist, bpm, key, color, etc. Update data-content-id attr. Update node.style.transform. Skip update if data-content-id matches (re-render not needed).
When a node scrolls out of visible+buffer window: remove from _visibleIndex; push back to _cardPool free-list (don't remove from DOM - just untag and let next renderItem reassign).
Selected-track state lives in module-level selectedTrackIds Set (existing). renderItem reads from this set and applies .selected class - so recycled nodes correctly show selection.
Reorder animations (Element.animate batched reads-then-writes) only fire for nodes in _visibleIndex. Off-screen 'movements' (filter reorders affecting hidden cards) skip animation entirely.
tests/web/virtualization.test.js: pool size never exceeds capacity; recycled node has updated content_id; selection state survives scroll.

technicalNotes: Existing _cardMap.smart-diffing fingerprint (TASK-035 covers this) determines when to do a full repool reset vs incremental update.
Memory: 40 * ~2KB/node = ~80KB DOM. The savings vs 10k * ~2KB = ~20MB DOM are significant - scroll perf gains follow.
